volumeTitle: Volume I: Publilius Syrus. Elegies on Maecenas. Grattius. Calpurnius Siculus. Laus Pisonis. Einsiedeln Eclogues. Aetna
label: Einsiedeln Eclogues (Section 1.1 - Section 2.30)

sections.label: Section 1.1
sections.original: Tityre , tu patulae recubans sub tegmine fagi
sections.translation: You , Tityrus , ' neath a broad beech-canopy

sections.label: Section 1.2
sections.original: silvestrem tenui Musam meditaris avena ;
sections.translation: reclining , on the slender oat rehearse

sections.label: Section 1.3
sections.original: nos patriae fines et dulcia linquimus arva :
sections.translation: your silvan ditties : I from my sweet fields ,

sections.label: Section 1.4
sections.original: nos patriam fugimus ; tu , Tityre , lentus in umbra
sections.translation: and home ' s familiar bounds , even now depart .

sections.label: Section 1.5
sections.original: formosam resonare doces Amaryllida silvas .
sections.translation: Exiled from home am I ; while , Tityrus , you

sections.label: Section 1.6
sections.original: O Meliboee , deus nobis haec otia fecit :
sections.translation: sit careless in the shade , and , at your call ,

sections.label: Section 1.7
sections.original: namque erit ille mihi semper deus ; illius aram
sections.translation: “ Fair Amaryllis ” bid the woods resound .

sections.label: Section 1.8
sections.original: saepe tener nostris ab ovilibus imbuet agnus .
sections.translation: O Meliboeus , ' twas a god vouchsafed

sections.label: Section 1.9
sections.original: Ille meas errare boves , ut cernis , et ipsum
sections.translation: this ease to us , for him a god will I

sections.label: Section 1.10
sections.original: ludere , quae vellem , calamo permisit agresti
sections.translation: deem ever , and from my folds a tender lamb

sections.label: Section 1.11
sections.original: Non equidem invideo ; miror magis : undique totis
sections.translation: oft with its life-blood shall his altar stain .

sections.label: Section 1.12
sections.original: usque adeo turbatur agris . En , ipse capellas
sections.translation: His gift it is that , as your eyes may see ,

sections.label: Section 1.13
sections.original: protinus aeger ago ; hanc etiam vix , Tityre , duco :
sections.translation: my kine may roam at large , and I myself

sections.label: Section 1.14
sections.original: hic inter densas corylos modo namque gemellos ,
sections.translation: play on my shepherd ' s pipe what songs I will .

sections.label: Section 1.15
sections.original: spem gregis , ah , silice in nuda conixa reliquit .
sections.translation: I grudge you not the boon , but marvel more ,

sections.label: Section 1.16
sections.original: Saepe malum hoc nobis , si mens non laeva fuisset ,
sections.translation: such wide confusion fills the country-side .

sections.label: Section 1.17
sections.original: de caelo tactas memini praedicere quercus :—
sections.translation: See , sick at heart I drive my she-goats on ,

sections.label: Section 1.18
sections.original: saepe sinistra cava praedixit ab ilice cornix .
sections.translation: and this one , O my Tityrus , scarce can lead :

sections.label: Section 1.19
sections.original: Sed tamen , iste deus qui sit , da , Tityre , nobis .
sections.translation: for ' mid the hazel-thicket here but now

sections.label: Section 1.20
sections.original: Urbem , quam dicunt Romam , Meliboee , putavi
sections.translation: she dropped her new-yeaned twins on the bare flint ,

sections.label: Section 1.21
sections.original: stultus ego huic nostrae similem , quo saepe solemus
sections.translation: hope of the flock — an ill , I mind me well ,

sections.label: Section 1.22
sections.original: pastores ovium teneros depellere fetus :
sections.translation: which many a time , but for my blinded sense ,

sections.label: Section 1.23
sections.original: sic canibus catulos similis , sic matribus haedos
sections.translation: the thunder-stricken oak foretold , oft too

sections.label: Section 1.24
sections.original: noram , sic parvis componere magna solebam :
sections.translation: from hollow trunk the raven ' s ominous cry .

sections.label: Section 1.25
sections.original: verum haec tantum alias inter caput extulit urbes ,
sections.translation: But who this god of yours ? Come , Tityrus , tell .

sections.label: Section 1.26
sections.original: quantum lenta solent inter viburna cupressi .
sections.translation: The city , Meliboeus , they call Rome ,

sections.label: Section 1.27
sections.original: Et quae tanta fuit Romam tibi causa videndi ?
sections.translation: I , simpleton , deemed like this town of ours ,

sections.label: Section 1.28
sections.original: Libertas ; quae sera , tamen respexit inertem ,
sections.translation: whereto we shepherds oft are wont to drive

sections.label: Section 1.29
sections.original: candidior postquam tondenti barba cadebat ;
sections.translation: the younglings of the flock : so too I knew

sections.label: Section 1.30
sections.original: respexit tamen , et longo post tempore venit ,
sections.translation: whelps to resemble dogs , and kids their dams ,

sections.label: Section 2.1
sections.original: Formosum pastor Corydon ardebat Alexim ,
sections.translation: the shepherd Corydon with love was fired

sections.label: Section 2.2
sections.original: delicias domini , nec quid speraret habebat ;
sections.translation: for fair Alexis , his own master ' s joy :

sections.label: Section 2.3
sections.original: tantum inter densas , umbrosa cacumina , fagos
sections.translation: no room for hope had he , yet , none the less ,

sections.label: Section 2.4
sections.original: adsidue veniebat . Ibi haec incondita solus
sections.translation: the thick-leaved shadowy-soaring beech-tree grove

sections.label: Section 2.5
sections.original: montibus et silvis studio iactabat inani :
sections.translation: still would he haunt , and there alone , as thus ,

sections.label: Section 2.6
sections.original: O crudelis Alexi , nihil mea carmina curas ?
sections.translation: to woods and hills pour forth his artless strains .

sections.label: Section 2.7
sections.original: Nil nostri miserere ? Mori me denique coges .
sections.translation: “ Cruel Alexis , heed you naught my songs ?

sections.label: Section 2.8
sections.original: nunc etiam pecudes umbras et frigora captant ;
sections.translation: Have you no pity ? you ' ll drive me to my death .

sections.label: Section 2.9
sections.original: nunc viridis etiam occultant spineta lacertos ,
sections.translation: Now even the cattle court the cooling shade

sections.label: Section 2.10
sections.original: Thestylis et rapido fessis messoribus aestu
sections.translation: and the green lizard hides him in the thorn :

sections.label: Section 2.11
sections.original: alia serpyllumque herbas contundit olentis .
sections.translation: now for tired mowers , with the fierce heat spent ,

sections.label: Section 2.12
sections.original: at mecum raucis , tua dum vestigia lustro ,
sections.translation: pounds Thestilis her mess of savoury herbs ,

sections.label: Section 2.13
sections.original: sole sub ardenti resonant arbusta cicadis .
sections.translation: wild thyme and garlic . I , with none beside ,

sections.label: Section 2.14
sections.original: Nonne fuit satius tristis Amaryllidis iras
sections.translation: save hoarse cicalas shrilling through the brake ,

sections.label: Section 2.15
sections.original: atque superba pati fastidia , nonne Menalcan ,
sections.translation: still track your footprints ' neath the broiling sun .

sections.label: Section 2.16
sections.original: quam vis ille niger , quamvis tu candidus esses ?
sections.translation: Better have borne the petulant proud disdain

sections.label: Section 2.17
sections.original: o formose puer , nimium ne crede colori !
sections.translation: of Amaryllis , or Menalcas wooed ,

sections.label: Section 2.18
sections.original: alba ligustra cadunt , vaccinia nigra leguntur .
sections.translation: albeit he was so dark , and you so fair !

sections.label: Section 2.19
sections.original: Despectus tibi sum , nec qui sim quaeris , Alexi ,
sections.translation: Trust not too much to colour , beauteous boy ;

sections.label: Section 2.20
sections.original: quam dives pecoris , nivei quam lactis abundans .
sections.translation: white privets fall , dark hyacinths are culled .

sections.label: Section 2.21
sections.original: mille meae Siculis errant in montibus agnae ;
sections.translation: You scorn me , Alexis , who or what I am

sections.label: Section 2.22
sections.original: lac mihi non aestate novum , non frigore defit ;
sections.translation: care not to ask — how rich in flocks , or how

sections.label: Section 2.23
sections.original: canto quae solitus , si quando armenta vocabat ,
sections.translation: in snow-white milk abounding : yet for me

sections.label: Section 2.24
sections.original: Amphion Dircaeus in Actaeo Aracimtho .
sections.translation: roam on Sicilian hills a thousand lambs ;

sections.label: Section 2.25
sections.original: Nec sum adeo informis : nuper me in litore vidi ,
sections.translation: summer or winter , still my milk-pails brim .

sections.label: Section 2.26
sections.original: cum placidum ventis staret mare ; non ego Daphnim
sections.translation: I sing as erst Amphion of Circe sang ,

sections.label: Section 2.27
sections.original: iudice te metuam , si numquam fallit imago .
sections.translation: what time he went to call his cattle home

sections.label: Section 2.28
sections.original: O tantum libeat mecum tibi sordida rura
sections.translation: on Attic Aracynthus . Nor am I

sections.label: Section 2.29
sections.original: atque humilis habitare casas , et figere cervos ,
sections.translation: so ill to look on : lately on the beach

sections.label: Section 2.30
sections.original: haedorumque gregem viridi compellere hibisco !
sections.translation: I saw myself , when winds had stilled the sea ,
